level_signals: Comp range $87K-$128K base consistent with mid-level individual contributor

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 2+ years of experience in management consulting / tech / design / investment banking / corporate strategy / business development / business operations or related experience
You have an established track record of achieving tangible outcomes and moving needles
You have experience leading projects, working in a team, and motivating people at all levels across a variety of job responsibilities
You can translate high-level goals into applicable plans & action items
You have excellent Excel skills (can perform complex functions)

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: high-growth or early-stage startup experience strongly preferred

scope.surface: Going Out product — driving DoorDash customers into physical partner stores; includes in-store discounts, VIP experiences, reservations, payments
scope.business_model: B2B2C marketplace; merchant partnerships driving in-store consumer traffic
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer engagement with Going Out product
In-store sales driven to merchant partners
core_responsibilities: Own merchant go-to-market strategy including sales GTM
Develop strategies to drive merchant volume and value
Own merchant sales and onboarding and merchant experience
Design and execute experiments from activation to optimization
Execute marketing and comms plans to grow customer base
Build models to evaluate success and set new goals
Collaborate with Product, Engineering, Operations, and Sales to scale Going Out

green_flags: Clear ownership of high-visibility new product ('Going Out')
Explicitly described as startup-like culture within a large company — good signal for autonomy
Position fill date disclosed (8/7/2026), suggesting active and time-bound hiring
Comprehensive benefits package described including 401(k), parental leave, wellness
red_flags: Work location/remote policy is not stated in the JD — unclear if remote, hybrid, or onsite
Role scope is broad (GTM, reporting, experiments, marketing, customer research) which may indicate under-staffed team
Pay range is wide ($41K spread) which may indicate flexibility but also ambiguity in leveling
